fr.recommendations: Enhancing the main building's areas of entry with clear signage and maintaining unobstructed pathways throughout could facilitate responder navigation and improve access effectiveness. Reducing the density of vegetation near key thoroughfares and entry points may enhance visibility and support safer movements across the property. Ensuring there is a reliable access pathway to the rear of the property could assist in operational readiness and the flexibility of emergency staging. Strategic clearing around the perimeter fencing might help establish better sightlines and enable more efficient responder deployment across the property.

wildfire.recommendations: Reducing the density of vegetation adjacent to the structure and increasing separation between vegetation clusters and the building could enhance defensible space and lower exposure to wildfire. Managing the tree cluster to lessen fuel continuity and ensuring that vegetated perimeters are maintained with breaks or open spaces may help to prevent fire spread. Addressing the access route and expanding it if feasible, while reducing excess vegetation along the pathway, could improve access for firefighters and ease suppression efforts. Creating or enhancing available turnaround areas would further support effective emergency response access. Consistent vegetation management around the property, particularly focusing on areas with limited access, can contribute to long-term resilience and enhance property preparedness against wildfires.
